automation.title: Automation Rules
automation.description: Create rules to automate repetitive tasks and keep your project moving.
automation.content_what: Automation rules let you define triggers, conditions, and actions. When a trigger event occurs and conditions are met, the actions execute automatically — saving you from manual, repetitive work.
automation.content_triggers: Choose when the rule should fire. Common triggers include status changes, priority updates, assignee changes, due date approaching, and task creation.
automation.trigger_event_desc: The event that starts the automation. E.g., "Status changed" fires when a task moves between columns.
automation.conditions_desc: Optional filters that narrow when the rule fires. E.g., only fire when priority is "High" or status is "In Progress".
automation.actions_desc: What happens when the trigger fires and conditions match. E.g., send notification, add label, change status.
automation.priority_desc: Rules are evaluated in priority order. Lower numbers run first. If multiple rules match, all will execute.
automation.tip_1: Start simple — one trigger, one action. Add complexity as needed.
automation.tip_2: Use conditions to prevent unnecessary automation runs.
automation.tip_3: Test rules by creating a test task and manually triggering the event.
automation.tip_4: Check activity logs to verify rule execution.

sla.title: SLA Policies
sla.description: Set response and resolution time targets for your tasks.
sla.content_what: SLA (Service Level Agreement) policies define time targets for responding to and resolving tasks. They help ensure your team meets agreed-upon service levels.
sla.content_breach: The system checks for SLA breaches hourly via a background job. When a breach is detected, a TaskActivity record is created and the task is flagged.
sla.response_time_desc: The maximum time allowed before the first response to a task. Measured from task creation.
sla.resolution_time_desc: The maximum time allowed to complete/resolve a task. Measured from task creation.
sla.breach_detection_desc: Automatically detects when tasks exceed their SLA targets. Breaches are logged in the task activity feed.
sla.policy_name_desc: A descriptive name for the SLA policy, e.g., "Critical Issue Response".
sla.tip_1: Define different SLA policies for different priority levels.
sla.tip_2: Set response time shorter than resolution time.
sla.tip_3: Monitor breach reports to identify bottlenecks in your workflow.
sla.tip_4: Combine SLA policies with automation rules for proactive notifications.

sprint.title: Sprint Planning
sprint.description: Manage time-boxed iterations to organize and track work in focused cycles.
sprint.content_what: A Sprint is a fixed time period (usually 1-4 weeks) where the team commits to completing a set of tasks. Sprints help you plan work, track progress, and deliver incrementally.
sprint.content_lifecycle:
1. Planned → Create a sprint and add tasks from the backlog.
2. Active → Start the sprint. Team works on the tasks.
3. Completed → Close the sprint when the time period ends.

Only one sprint can be active at a time.
sprint.start_sprint_desc: Moves the sprint to "active" status. Only available when no other sprint is active. Sets the start date to today.
sprint.close_sprint_desc: Moves the sprint to "completed" status. Sets the completion date. Incomplete tasks remain in the sprint but are no longer actively tracked.
sprint.add_task_desc: Adds an existing task to this sprint. Tasks can be added from the Backlog page or from the task detail view.
sprint.remove_task_desc: Removes a task from the sprint. The task is not deleted — it goes back to the backlog.
sprint.view_report_desc: Opens the sprint report showing burndown chart, velocity, and completion stats.
sprint.tip_1: Plan your sprint by adding tasks from the Backlog before starting it.
sprint.tip_2: Use story points to estimate effort and track velocity across sprints.
sprint.tip_3: Check the sprint report after closing to see how the team performed.
sprint.tip_4: Incomplete tasks can be moved to the next sprint or left in the backlog.

board.title: Kanban Board
board.description: Visualize your workflow by dragging tasks across columns to reflect their current status.
board.content_how: Each column represents a stage in your workflow (e.g., To Do, In Progress, Done). Tasks are displayed as cards that you can drag between columns to update their status.
board.content_realtime: See who else is viewing the board via the avatars in the header. Changes made by others appear instantly without page reload.
board.drag_drop_desc: Drag a task card from one column to another to change its status. The move is saved automatically.
board.column_management_desc: Click "Columns" to add, rename, reorder, or delete board columns. You can also set WIP (Work In Progress) limits.
board.swimlanes_desc: Group tasks by assignee, priority, or epic using the swimlane dropdown. Helps visualize workload distribution.
board.multiple_boards_desc: Switch between different board views using the board selector in the header. Each board can have its own columns.
board.tip_1: Use keyboard shortcut "c" to quickly create a new task from the board.
board.tip_2: Click a task card to open the detail drawer without leaving the board.
board.tip_3: Set WIP limits on columns to prevent overloading your team.
board.tip_4: Use swimlanes to spot bottlenecks in specific areas of work.

backlog.title: Backlog Management
backlog.description: Organize and prioritize tasks that are not yet assigned to a sprint.
backlog.content_what: The backlog is a holding area for all tasks that haven't been assigned to a sprint. It's your project's task inbox where you can triage, prioritize, and organize work before committing it to a sprint.
backlog.content_planning: When planning a sprint, drag tasks from the backlog into a sprint to commit them to that iteration. Tasks in the backlog are visible but not actively worked on.
backlog.drag_reorder_desc: Drag tasks within the backlog to reorder them by priority. Tasks at the top are typically worked on first.
backlog.add_to_sprint_desc: Click the sprint dropdown on a task to assign it to a specific sprint. The task moves from backlog to that sprint.
backlog.sprint_overview_desc: See all sprints and their task counts in the Sprints section. Click a sprint name to view its details.
backlog.task_details_desc: Click a task to open the detail drawer. From there you can edit properties, add comments, or move it to a sprint.
backlog.tip_1: Keep the backlog groomed — regularly review and reprioritize tasks.
backlog.tip_2: Use story points to estimate effort before adding tasks to sprints.
backlog.tip_3: Tasks at the top of the backlog are highest priority.
backlog.tip_4: Create tasks directly from the backlog if you're not ready to assign them to a sprint.

approval.title: Approval Flows
approval.description: Set up approval gates that require team sign-off before tasks can move to certain columns.
approval.content_what: Approval flows create mandatory review steps in your workflow. When a task reaches a column with an active approval flow, it cannot proceed until the required number of approvals are received.
approval.content_how: When someone tries to move a task to a column with an approval flow, the system creates pending approval requests for the designated approvers. The task can only move once the minimum number of approvals is met.
approval.column_trigger_desc: Each flow is tied to a specific board column. When a task is moved to that column, the approval process begins automatically.
approval.approvers_desc: Specify who can approve — individual users by email, or roles like "QA Lead" or "Project Manager". Role-based approvers resolve to all users with that role.
approval.min_approvals_desc: Set how many approvals are required before the task can proceed. For example, require 2 out of 3 designated approvers to sign off.
approval.enable_disable_desc: Toggle flows on or off without deleting them. Disabled flows are ignored when tasks move to the column.
approval.tip_1: Use approval flows for quality-critical columns like "Ready for QA" or "Needs Review".
approval.tip_2: Combine with WIP limits to control both quality and throughput.
approval.tip_3: Role-based approvers are more flexible than user-based — new team members automatically become eligible.
approval.tip_4: Keep approval flows lightweight — too many gates can slow down your workflow.

reports.title: Project Reports
reports.description: Track project health with velocity charts, burndown tracking, and workload distribution.
reports.content_overview: Reports provide a snapshot of your project's progress. View task completion rates, velocity trends, burndown charts, and team workload distribution.
reports.content_planning: Use velocity data to estimate how much work your team can handle in future sprints. Burndown charts show if you're on track to complete sprint goals.
reports.summary_cards_desc: Quick overview of total tasks, completed count, overdue tasks, and overall completion rate.
reports.tasks_by_status_desc: Bar chart showing task distribution across workflow stages. Helps identify bottlenecks.
reports.velocity_chart_desc: Bar chart comparing committed vs completed story points across sprints. Shows your team's average velocity.
reports.burndown_chart_desc: Line chart showing remaining work vs ideal burndown for the current sprint. Helps predict if you'll finish on time.
reports.team_workload_desc: Shows each team member's assigned tasks and completion count. Helps identify overloaded team members.
reports.tip_1: Check velocity after each sprint to improve future sprint planning accuracy.
reports.tip_2: A healthy burndown shows a steady downward trend — flat spots indicate blocked work.
reports.tip_3: Use workload data to rebalance assignments if someone is overloaded.
reports.tip_4: Review reports weekly to catch issues early before they become blockers.

github.title: GitHub
github.push_commits: Push commits to your default branch with
github.auto_complete: in commit messages to auto-complete tasks.
github.pr_linking: Mention task codes in PR titles to link pull requests.
github.setup_description: Connect your project to GitHub to link commits and pull requests to tasks.
